response: The Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT), founded in 1861, is ranked the #1 university in the world, with a highly selective acceptance rate of just 3.9%. It enrolls approximately 11,500 students and employs 1,069 faculty members, boasting an impressive 98 Nobel laureates among its affiliates. With an endowment of $24.6 billion and annual tuition of $57,590, MIT remains a global leader in education and research.
